strengths: Extensive full-stack experience significantly exceeding requirement (14 years vs 6+); currently Staff engineer with TypeScript/Node.js explicitly documented in current role
Currently contributing to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, exceeding stated requirement for AI agent development experience
Demonstrated testing culture and TDD practice across multiple roles; MIT CS degree and willingness to teach/mentor others
concerns: Nest.js not mentioned despite being primary stack; resume shows Node.js expertise but not Nest.js-specific patterns or experience
React not explicitly documented; recent frontend work not visible—Ember.js mentioned in older roles, but primary stack requires React
Authentication, authorization, and security best-practices knowledge not evident in resume; missing from skill list and employment descriptions

key_factors.factor: Nest.js, React, Vitest not explicitly demonstrated
Experience level significantly exceeds mid-senior requirement

response.justification: Szabo significantly exceeds the experience requirement (14 years vs 6+, currently Staff vs Mid-Senior) and demonstrates core TypeScript/Node.js expertise across current and prior roles. His current AI-agent infrastructure work matches the stated requirement. However, primary-stack technologies lack explicit documentation: Nest.js is absent (though Node.js mastery is proven), React is not referenced despite being on the primary stack, and testing-tool specificity (Vitest/Jest) is not demonstrated despite strong TDD culture. Authentication/authorization knowledge is not evident. These gaps may reflect resume incompleteness or represent areas requiring development; his demonstrated ability to work across languages (D, Java, C++, Swift) suggests strong learning agility. Interview should clarify React/Nest.js/security knowledge.
